summary.intent: Investigate issue CHA-9 (P1.2: Define CRM types) by understanding the existing CRM crate architecture and posting a structured investigation summary to Linear.
summary.outcome: The investigation stage ran but also produced code changes across the CRM crate, including new type definitions, store interfaces, SQLite/memory store implementations, migrations, and error handling — going beyond the investigation-only scope.

repo: Project uses a Rust workspace with crates organized under crates/, including a dedicated crm crate
CRM crate follows a layered architecture: types.rs, store.rs (trait), store_sqlite.rs, store_memory.rs, error.rs
Database migrations are stored in crates/crm/migrations/ with timestamp-prefixed SQL files
The crate uses SQLite as its primary store with an in-memory store for testing

code.finding: CRM domain types defined here — likely includes Contact, Organization, or similar CRM entities
Abstract store trait defining the CRM persistence interface
SQLite-backed implementation of the store trait
In-memory store implementation, likely used for tests
Migration created on 2026-03-17 to define CRM schema tables

workflow: Agent workflow uses Linear comments as a persistent workpad for tracking investigation status and decisions
Session startup requires running type-check and tests before any implementation to establish a clean baseline
Investigation stage is supposed to be read-only (no code changes), but this session produced file modifications — the agent overstepped its stage boundaries

friction: Agent violated the investigation stage constraint by modifying source files — the prompt explicitly says 'Do NOT write implementation code or modify source files'
Issue CHA-9 had no description provided, making it harder to scope the investigation correctly
Issue labels and current status fields were blank in the lifecycle context, reducing available signal for scoping
open_items: Verify that the types defined in types.rs and the migration schema align with the actual CRM domain requirements (no description was available to validate against)
The investigation summary and workpad comment that should have been posted to Linear may not reflect the actual implementation decisions made
Review whether the store trait abstraction and SQLite implementation are complete or placeholders needing further work in a later stage
Cargo.lock was modified — confirm dependency additions are intentional and minimal
